seoTitle: Royal George vs Runnymede — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Royal George and Runnymede. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Royal George vs Runnymede: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Royal George and Runnymede.
heading: Royal George vs Runnymede — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Royal George, Tasmania and Runnymede, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Royal George has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Runnymede (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Royal George or Runnymede safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Royal George has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Runnymede scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Royal George scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Runnymede scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Royal George or Runnymede?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Royal George has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
